page.content: In the first table, titled "Experimental group" with corresponding "Antibody titers against H1 virus (HI)" for "Day 7" and "Day 14," the data reveals the following: for the experimental group rLH5-H1N1, antibody titers are reported as <40 on both Day 7 and Day 14. The group rLH5 + IL-2-H1N1 also shows antibody titers of <40 for both days. In contrast, the H1N1-H1N1 group demonstrates significant antibody titers of 320 on Day 7 and 640 on Day 14, while the PBS-H1N1 group is marked as n.d. for both days.

page.content: Finally, the IL-2-H1N1 group shows <40 on Day 7 and n.d. on Day 14. The second table, labeled "GI conditions," presents data on patients and their corresponding percent representation. The "LM" condition includes 94 patients, representing 21.4%, while "FM" has 31 patients at 7.1%. The "LM + FM" condition comprises 27 patients at 6.2%. The "HI" condition involves 100 patients, making up 22.8%, with "LM + HI" at 52 patients, or 11.8%. The "FM + HI" condition includes 23 patients, representing 5.2%, and "LM + FM + HI" accounts for 14 patients at 3.2%. The total across all conditions is 341 patients, representing 77.7%.
